items.title: Thai opposition figures face trial over attempt to change royal defamation law
items.description: Forty-four lawmakers could be banned from office for seeking to change Thailand's strict lese-majeste law, which criminalizes criticism of the monarchy. Critics say it has been used to silence opponents.

items.title: Teenager becomes first Congo virus fatality in Pakistan's Karachi
items.description: Concern among health officials in Karachi with the festival of Id al-Azha due next month

items.title: INDEC: Economic activity in Argentina fell 2.6% in February
items.description: Economic activity posts steepest drop since December 2023, INDEC says; Manufacturing and commerce lead declines in February, gains for mining and agriculture. Leer más

items.title: Chinese Foreign Minister and Thai Prime Minister agree to collaborate on fighting cyberscams
items.description: China is Thailand’s biggest trading partner, and the two countries celebrated their 50th anniversary of establishing diplomatic relations last year

items.title: SpaceX IPO filing shows Elon Musk can retain board control
items.description: A 2024 study by the National Association of Corporate Directors found that just 3% to 4% of the Russell 3000 index was comprised of firms where insiders constituted ​a board majority

items.title: Trump says U.S. in no rush but 'clock is ticking' for Iran
items.description: Mr. Trump, who on April 23, 2026, ruled out the use of a nuclear weapon against Iran, had earlier ordered the U.S. Navy to destroy any Iranian boat caught laying mines in Hormuz

items.title: Why the gas tax momentum is building to stop selling Australia short
items.description: Gas giants are breathing a sigh of relief after Prime Minister Anthony Albanese ruled out a 25 per cent tax on exports in next month's budget. But for how long will the federal government resist something that one leading expert says is in our "national interest"?

items.title: Warner Bros.-Paramount deal advances
items.description: The Warner Bros. takeover saga took another turn on Thursday and may finally be nearing its conclusion, with shareholders approving a buyout at $31 per share from Paramount Skydance, although regulators still need to review the deal that would merge two major entertainment groups, amid opposition, particularly from Hollywood, concerned about its potential impact on media plurality, as Luke Shrago reports.

items.title: Live: Winless Tigers pushing hard against Demons in Anzac Day Eve clash
items.description: Richmond and Melbourne meet on the MCG stage for their now-traditional Anzac Day Eve clash, as the Tigers look to get off the mark against a dangerous Demons side. Follow live.

items.title: Tensions rise in two ceasefires in the Middle East. And, the Navy secretary ousted
items.description: Tensions are rising in the Middle East as shaky ceasefire agreements between the U.S., Israel and Iran, and Lebanon and Israel, are tested. And, the Secretary of the Navy is out of the role.

items.title: US boards ship carrying Iran oil as Trump threatens mine-laying vessels
items.description: Central Command said it has intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran.

items.title: Lebanon
items.description: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend a ceasefire for three weeks, according to the White House, which hosted ambassadors from both countries, but fighting persists as Hezbollah, absent from the talks, has launched new waves of rockets and drones, while Israeli forces continue operations in southern Lebanon and strike Hezbollah targets, as Washington correspondent Fraser Jackson reported earlier.
items.source: Israel: Truce extended by three weeks

items.title: Environmental crisis in Russian city after drone attack on refinery
items.description: People in the Russian city of Tuapse are worried about the effects of an oil refinery fire after a Ukrainian attack.

items.title: New mega-bridge in Lesotho to double water exports to South Africa
items.description: Every time someone in Johannesburg drinks a glass of water, there is a 60% chance it comes from neighbouring Lesotho. Now, a newly built bridge will help the tiny mountain kingdom nearly double those exports.

items.title: Israel, Lebanon extend ceasefire as Trump says ‘great chance’ for peace deal

US President Donald Trump said he believed there was a "great chance" the two countries would agree on a peace deal, even though Israel and Hezbollah have accused each other of multiple violations of the original truce.

items.title: Paredes penalty ensures Superclásico seniority for Boca

items.description: Delaying the closure of South Africa’s coal-fired power plants could lead to up to 32,000 preventable deaths between 2026 and 2050, climate rights groups warned on Wednesday.

items.title: How far would you go for a vanilla slice?

items.title: Trump orders navy to shoot Iranian boats
items.description: The standoff between the United States and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz appears to be edging closer to renewed conflict than a peace deal, with President Donald Trump ordering the Navy to target Iranian boats laying mines and Israel signaling it is ready to resume bombing once it receives the go-ahead from Washington, as Oliver Farry from our international affairs desk explains.

items.title: Canada's AI startup Cohere buys Germany's Aleph Alpha to expand in Europe

items.title: Mass trial of MS-13 gang at CECOT prison
items.description: El Salvador has launched a mass trial of 486 alleged MS-13 members accused by prosecutors of thousands of murders and other crimes, as President Nayib Bukele continues his sweeping crackdown under a state of emergency that has sharply reduced crime, though human rights groups warn the measures—introduced in 2022—have led to serious abuses, including violations of due process, reports of torture, and more than 500 deaths in prison, as reported by Caroline Baum.

items.title: Hundreds of wildfires burn across Florida and Georgia
items.description: Officials in both states cite dry conditions, high winds and drought as key factors making the fires difficult to control.

items.title: Eye on Africa: Tanzania poll violence report
items.description: The commission of inquiry into electoral violence in Tanzania released its report on Thursday, a document commissioned by President Samia Suluhu, re-elected with 97.66% of the vote, which has been rejected by the opposition but acknowledges that hundreds of demonstrators were killed in the aftermath of the presidential election, as reported by regional correspondent Bastien Renouil.

items.title: 'No hardliners or moderates': Iran denies US claims of fragmented leadership
items.description: Top Iranian officials took to social media on Thursday to push back against US claims that its leadership is divided. ‘There are no hardliners or moderates,’ the country’s president and parliamentary speaker both posted.

items.description: Even as the U.S. threatens military action, Cuba's ambassador to Mexico blames Washington for the country's crisis and says Cuba is prepared for confrontation if diplomacy collapses.

items.title: Watch: Above 92% turnout in Bengal, 85% in Tamil Nadu amid poll violence | Above the Fold | 23.04.2026
items.description: In today’s episode, we look at the key election trends in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal, tracking major shifts ahead of polling. We also cover the political row in the United States over birthright citizenship, rising tensions in West Asia around the Strait of Hormuz, and a significant change in West Bengal’s campaign landscape, and more

items.title: What is Europe's plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz?
items.description: France and the UK are leading efforts to establish a multinational mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Questions remain about the scope of the operation and whether it can bring lasting stability.

items.title: Woman chooses to go blind in one eye after delays in care
items.description: A 47-year-old woman receiving care at a public eye clinic in South Australia says her experience has been so poor she is choosing to go blind in one eye.

items.title: Trump orders U.S. military to 'shoot and kill' Iranian small boats choking Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Middle East war live: EU must ‘step up efforts’ to restore stability, Macron says

items.title: 'Homophobia has no place': AFL boss rejects ruling on slurs in sport

items.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine

items.title: With travel chaos looming, Brittany Ferries vows no price hikes

Christophe Mathieu, CEO of Brittany Ferries, tells FRANCE 24 the line has enough supplies of maritime fuels to last the season, and vows not to raise ticket prices despite the global energy crisis. Also in the show: Warner Bros. shareholders vote to approve a takeover by Paramount Skydance, a mega-merger which could reshape the US media landscape.

items.title: Nepal: Mount Everest ice block obstructs large group of spring climbers

items.title: Trump, Iran both share false information about eight women facing execution
items.description: On Wednesday, Donald Trump claimed that out of respect for him, Iran had agreed to spare the lives of eight young Iranian women facing execution. But he was quickly struck with accusations that the images of the women were AI-generated. Iran itself gave conflicting information, both mocking the AI claims but also declaring that none of the women faced confirmed death sentences. The reality – as confirmed by two NGOs – is that all parties shared incorrect information.

items.title: Daniel Noboa’s ‘new Ecuador’ has some fearing loss of civil freedoms
items.description: Ecuador's Daniel Noboa appears to be consolidating power in ways that are causing growing unease in civil society. One week it’s political parties, the next it’s the courts. Now it’s the media. Leer más

items.title: After losing her son, healthy British woman will legally end her life in Swiss clinic
items.description: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, April 24: We look at reactions in the German, Russian and Kazakh press after an important oil pipeline from Russia is suspended to Germany. Russia says it's for "technical" reasons, but the German press say it is "political". Also: the press is divided over the case of a physically healthy, deeply grieving British woman who will die by euthanasia in a Swiss clinic this Friday. Finally, we look at the most surprising clauses of pre-nup agreements.

items.description: India's weather department has issued heatwave warnings in parts of northern India.

items.title: Miami lawyers consider Messi to be AFA ‘accomplice’ in multi-million lawsuit
items.description: United States-based lawyer claims Lionel Messi is an accomplice of the Argentine Football Association (AFA) in a multi-million lawsuit for breach of contract. Leer más

items.title: UK, France agree 3‑year deal to curb Channel crossings
items.description: For the first time, the deal links some UK payments to France to performance in stopping migrants. Funding will be halted after one year if targets are not met.

items.title: Milei receives Israel’s highest civilian honour from president
items.description: Israeli President Isaac Herzog awards Argentina’s President Javier Milei the ‘Presidential Medal of Honour’ – the country’s highest civilian distinction – in recognition of his commitment to the State of Israel. Leer más

items.title: Germany unveils first ever military strategy for Bundeswehr
items.description: For the first time in its history, the Bundeswehr has adopted a military strategy to prepare Germany for future threats. Large sections of the document are classified.

items.title: Pentagon email floats suspending Spain from NATO, other steps over Iran rift, source says
items.description: One option in the email envisions suspending “difficult” countries from important or prestigious positions at NATO, a U.S. official claims.

items.title: Maradona's daughter slams 'manipulation' of family by his doctors at trial
items.description: Gianinna Maradona alleges in court that her father's medical team engaged in “total and horrible” manipulation. Daughter of ‘El Diez’ is the first witness in second trial. Leer más

items.title: Israeli ambassador: 'Only a strong Israel can achieve peace'
items.description: In an interview with DW, Ron Prosor, Israel's ambassador to Germany, defends his country's military strikes against Iran and Lebanon. He also advises Germany's Chancellor Friedrich Merz to show more "tact."

items.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 56 after Trump extended ceasefire?
items.description: The Israel-Lebanon ceasefire is extended by three weeks after White House talks with Israeli and Lebanese envoys.

items.title: Russia to block Kazakh oil flows to Germany via key pipeline
items.description: Germany has confirmed that Russia will block flows of Kazakh oil to a vital refinery that supplies much of Berlin's energy needs from May 1.

items.title: Could Iran war trigger bigger trade crisis than COVID?
items.description: As the Iran war simmers, trade experts ask whether it could reshape supply chains more deeply than COVID-19, forcing companies to rethink strategies to counter further geopolitical shocks.

items.title: China’s DeepSeek unveils latest models a year after upending global tech
items.description: Chinese startup says DeepSeek-V4-Pro beats all rival open models for maths and coding.

items.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
items.description: Following the return of Russia to the Biennale, the EU said it will carry out its threat to cut its funding from the international art show.

items.title: Trump envoy asks FIFA to replace Iran with Italy at World Cup
items.description: An envoy to United States President Donald Trump world football's governing body FIFA to replace Iran with Italy at the upcoming World Cup, the Financial Times reported on Wednesday.

items.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
items.description: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

items.title: News24 | Iran slams US ‘blatant violation of the ceasefire’, vows to keep Strait of Hormuz closed
items.description: Iran’s vow not to reopen the Strait of Hormuz - so long as a US naval blockade remained in place - was held into Thursday despite a ceasefire extension.

items.title: Philippines' ex-president Duterte to face ICC trial for crimes against humanity
items.description: The Philippines’ former president Rodrigo Duterte will face trail at the International Criminal Court in The Hague. Judges on Thursday confirmed charges of crimes against humanity against Duterte for dozens of alleged extrajudicial killings.

items.title: Tanzania poll violence claimed 518 lives: government enquiry
items.description: Violence around Tanzania’s October general election left 518 people dead, a government-appointed commission said Thursday — a figure far below opposition estimates of thousands killed.

items.title: Pakistan military kills 22 in northwest clashes, child dead in crossfire
items.description: "A joint military operation was conducted by security forces and law enforcement agencies" that resulted in "intense exchange of fire" in which the militants were killed, Says military media wing statement

items.title: YPF investors say they’ll arbitrate $16-billion Argentina case
items.description: Former YPF investors who saw US$16.1-billion US judgment against Argentina thrown out on appeal last month tell trial judge that they intend to pursue international treaty arbitration and further appeals. Leer más

items.title: South Africa inflation ticks higher as education and housing costs rise
items.description: South Africa’s inflation rate edged slightly higher in March but remains relatively subdued overall.

items.title: Relatives of 10 scientists who died or vanished grapple with impact of wild speculation
items.description: Conspiracies are running rampant online after the deaths or disappearances of at least 10 people tied to sensitive US research.

items.title: Baby twins attend their father’s funeral after Israeli settler attack
items.description: Palestinians gathered in the occupied West Bank to bury a 25-year-old father of twins shot dead by Israeli settlers.

items.title: Eye on Africa: Fally Ipupa marks 20 years of career
items.description: Congolese music star Fally Ipupa is marking 20 years in the industry with new albums and major concerts in Paris, celebrating a journey that took him from Kinshasa to becoming one of Africa’s most recognized artists, known for pioneering a modern take on Congolese rumba by blending it with R&B, pop and urban sounds. Clarisse Fortuné reports.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Bangladesh Cricket Board withdraws Mustafizur's NOC for PSL
items.description: "The Bangladesh Cricket Board wishes to inform that, following the conclusion of the 3rd ODI against New Zealand, the team's medical staff has reviewed the condition of national team pace bowler Mr. Mustafizur Rahman," says the BCB

items.title: Kenyan leader sparks uproar after  mocking Nigerians' spoken English
items.description: William Ruto says Kenyans speak "some of the best English in the world" while it is hard to understand Nigerians.

items.title: News24 | Soldier busted for betting on US capture of Venezuela’s Nicolas Maduro, making $400 000
items.description: A US Army soldier involved in the capture of Nicolas Maduro has been charged with making $400 000 ‌by betting on the removal of the ousted Venezuelan leader, the Justice Department said.

items.title: News24 | Singer D4vd had child sex abuse images on phone, prosecutor says in teen murder case
items.description: D4vd had sexual abuse content on his mobile phone, a US prosecutor said Thursday.

items.title: Djibouti's Guelleh reelected for sixth presidential term: official results
items.description: Ismail Omar Guelleh has won a sixth term as President of Djibouti, the country's constitutional council has confirmed. Guelleh won 97.01% of the vote, the council said on Tuesday.

items.title: News24 | ANALYSIS | Why minerals-for-security deals won’t save the DRC
items.description: Resource bartering doesn’t deliver stability – it perpetuates institutional fragility and rarely builds public trust and legitimacy in African governments, write Bram Verelst, Said Abdullahi and Veronica Chepseba in ISS Today.

items.title: My five-minute phone call with President Trump
items.description: Sarah Smith asked the president about King Charles's upcoming visit, the state of the so-called special relationship and the war with Iran.

items.title: Russia's Vladimir Putin hosts Seychelles President Patrick Herminie at Kremlin
items.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met Seychelles President Patrick Herminie at the Kremlin on Wednesday for talks focused on bilateral ties and international issues.

items.title: Luis Puenzo, director of Argentina’s first Oscar-winning feature, dies at 80
items.description: Luis Puenzo, director who won Argentina’s first Oscar, has died aged 80; 'La historia oficial,' which tackled kidnapping of infants during dictatorship era, won the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film in 1986. Leer más

items.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire extended by 3 weeks, says Trump
items.description: The White House meeting was the second high-level negotiation between Israel and Lebanon since last week

items.title: News24 | Trump administration proposes Congo resettlement for 1 100 Afghan allies denied US visas
items.description: The Trump administration is in talks with the Democratic Republic of Congo to resettle 1 100 Afghans who have been stranded in Qatar awaiting ‌US visas, according to an advocacy organisation that works on their behalf.

items.title: Israel-Iran war updates: Trump says Israel and Lebanon agree to extend ceasefire by 3 weeks
items.description: Earlier, blasts were reported over Tehran and air defences activated, although Israel claimed it was not attacking Iran

items.title: Africa faces 86 million tonne fuel shortfall by 2040: finance body
items.description: The Africa Finance Corporation says this highlights the continent's vulnerability to global shipping chokepoints.

items.title: Why is there a political deadlock in Lebanon?
items.description: The Lebanese government wants Hezbollah to disarm as it aims for a state monopoly on weapons.

items.title: Timberwolves take 2-1 NBA playoff lead over Nuggets, Hawks down Knicks
items.description: Elsewhere, Toronto Raptors beat Cleveland Cavaliers, snapping a 12-game playoff losing streak against the Cavs.

items.title: ICAC investigating senior SA police officer for alleged misconduct
items.description: The state's anti-corruption agency says the officer, who cannot be named, is under investigation to determine whether they "committed a breach of discipline contrary to the SA Police Code of Conduct".

items.title: Strait of Hormuz: Iran, US seize ships
items.description: US President Donald Trump says he is not rushing into a peace agreement with Iran, downplaying concerns over Iranian small boats even after they seized two container ships in the Strait of Hormuz, and arguing that Tehran is unable to strike a deal due to divisions within its leadership, an accusation Iran has rejected. Elitsa Gadeva tells us more.

items.title: In Algerian desert, Sahrawi refugees still dream of independent homeland
items.description: Sahrawi refugees go about daily life in the Aousserd camp near Tindouf in southern Algeria as residents say decades in exile have not weakened their demand for independence in Western Sahara: "Our demands are simple -- independence, and nothing else."

items.title: Amnesty warns of ‘predatory’ world order as leaders fuel crisis
items.description: Human rights group says global protection system built over last 80 years faces potential collapse as governments fail to act against abuses; Amnesty's chief warns Trump, Putin and Netanyahu are "predators" and must not be appeased. Leer más

items.title: Germany's finance minister wants to scrap spousal tax splitting
items.description: Highly controversial, but not well understood: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil wants to change the tax benefits for married couples.

items.title: Tokyo urges government workers to swap suits for shorts to counter energy costs
items.description: Government workers in Tokyo have new dress code rules that allow them to wear shorts to the office in a bid to cut air conditioning costs, an official said on Friday, as the Middle East war drives up energy costs around the world.

items.title: News24 | EU invites Zelensky to celebrate €90-billion Ukraine loan after Hungary roadblock removed
items.description: EU leaders will welcome Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in Cyprus to celebrate the release of a €90-billion loan for Kyiv after a prolonged deadlock.

items.title: Epstein housed abuse victims in London flats, BBC reveals
items.description: The revelations intensify concerns about repeated police decisions not to investigate 2015 trafficking claims.
